clean_text: Alhamdulillah. Subhanah tells us guide us the straight path. This is a verse that we recite every day in every of every salah. And asking for this is a condition in order for our prayer to be considered acceptable for our prayer's validity. What is what is the straight path? Guide us the straight path. Notice the curious wording. Guide us the straight path. Meaning, guide us to the straight path and guide us on the straight path and guide us in the straight path. Allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala he tells us that there is such a thing as the straight path of Allah. He refers to this path elsewhere as the path of Allah. What is this path? Our prophet sallall.aii wasallam has told us of this path and he has described it to us. There's a narration which was collected by immed on the authority of an in which the prophet sallall.aii said gives us a parable, an analogy of our path to him. Our entire life, every waking moment, from the point that you or I began to understand right from wrong, left from right, up from down, we have been invited to this path to embrace it, to stay in it. Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has guided us to this path from the inception from the moment he created Adam and breathed life into him because a human being without guidance is not just a lifeless being but a lifeless satellite. We've been designed to be guided. And if a person has that part missing, not only will their dunya suffer, not only will their a be at stake or lost, but they will always be incomplete. And so what is this straight path that Allah has described to us? Our prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam says in this narration, Allah has given us an analogy of our path to him and the path of guidance as a straight and broad open path. He says Allah gives an analogy of his path as a straight broad open path clear and no crookedness in it. And on both sides of this path, all the way down from the start to finish are walls. And in these two walls, on either side of the path, there are open doors. And on each of these open doors, there is a curtain that is hanging down and covering what's behind that door. So to understand the analogy, our prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam is describing to us a path that has two walls running down the sides of this path all the way down. And in these two walls, there are doors that are open, doorways you can walk through all the way down. And there are curtains hanging on each of these doors concealing what is behind them. He says, He says, "And at the entrance of this path, there is someone there calling and inviting everyone saying, "Oh people, enter into the path of Allah. together and do not become distracted or go crooked. Go where the path takes you. Do not deviate from this path. He says focus He says, "And above this path, there's a voice calling." Every time someone who is in this path gets curious about these open doors and starts to wander and contemplate moving the curtain over to see what's on the other side, this caller calls from above and says, "Be careful. Do not open that curtain. Don't look behind it. Don't interfere with it. Keep going straight because if you open that curtain and take a peek at what's behind, you're going to walk through that door. And immediately walking through that door is what? Walking off of the path of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. So notice here how we have this big broad inviting path. At the end of this path is Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. Your journey to Allah. And so as you're on this path in this analogy that our prophet gives us sallaihi wasallam every step you take on this path you're taking it whenever you implement a sunnah of the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam. In order to make way or make any kind of distance down this path, it is only by way of the sunnah. If you try to go down this path in your own way or in a way that Muhammad Sam did not take, you will not move an inch forward. Not one inch. No matter how much you run, you're running in place until you are walking the way that the prophet sallall.aii wasallam did. At the end of this path is your meeting with Allah. Wherever your life ends, that is where you will meet Allah. Some people will meet Allah very far down the path. And at the absolute furthest place down that path is Muhammad sallall.aii wasallam. And right behind him are the remainder of the prophets and messengers. Right behind them are Abu Bakr and Omar and Ali and the great companions of the prophets and messengers and so forth. Each person makes his distance down this path based on how closely he is following the prophet sallall.aii wasallam. You can't take a single step unless it is exactly how our prophet took it. If you try to walk down the path your own way, you'll be moving a lot, but you'll be treading water. Not one inch. And so the prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam then breaks this analogy down. He says saiam continuing in the analogy. Muslim. He says sallall.aii wasallam the path is Islam and the two walls on either side of the path those are the boundaries of Allah and those open doors they are the prohibitions of Allah. When a person does something haram they walk through one of those doors. And innately if you walk through one of those doors, you have walked off the path. He says, "And that caller that is above the path that is with you as you walk down that path, that every time someone wants to peek behind one of those curtains just to see what it's this disobedience of Allah, I don't want to go in. I just want to take a peek. I just want to see what's going on on the other side." You need to know, right? you can't live in a bubble kind of thing. He says that is the conscience of the Muslim. The conscience of the Muslim. That caller that every time you want to peek behind one of those curtains, he says, "Be careful. Do not peek behind that curtain because if you peek behind it, you're going to enter that door." That is the conscience of the Muslim in the heart of every believer. This is the path that we are asking of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala and this is the journey that we are on. But it's very important for us to realize that the Quran teaches us where the path is and how to tread it. But you cannot make a single inch in distance. You can't move forward a millimeter down that path unless you are doing it in the same footsteps that the prophet Muhammad took. Wasam. All subal subhanahuaala for Muhammad. them in.

summary: This lecture presents a detailed explanation of the verse "Guide us the straight path" (Ayat al-Istiqamah) from Surah Al-Fatiha, utilizing a prophetic hadith that describes the path of Allah through an extended analogy. The speaker explains that Allah's path is likened to a straight, broad, open road with walls on both sides containing doors with curtains. At the entrance, a caller invites people to enter and stay on the path, while another voice from above warns against deviating by opening the curtains (which represent forbidden acts). The speaker emphasizes that progression on this path depends entirely on following the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad, and that any attempt to proceed without adhering to his example results in no forward movement. The analogy is then broken down: the path represents Islam, the walls represent Allah's boundaries, the doors represent prohibitions (haram), and the voice above represents the Muslim's conscience.
suggested_title: The Straight Path: An Analysis of the Prophet's Hadith on Following the Sunnah
